page: Microsoft Azure E64ads_v7 is an Eadsv7-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 3780 GB disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPU accelerators are included.

Benchmark data indicates strong compute and memory capabilities. The AMD EPYC processor achieves top-tier performance in stress-ng single-core CPU tests and PassMark CPU scores, alongside strong multi-core performance. Memory subsystem metrics show top-tier PassMark memory scores and strong read and write bandwidth, reaching 205.73 GB/sec for large-block read operations. Database operations on PassMark rank in the top 10%, and Redis SET operations reach 103.04 million operations per second.

While the instance excels in single-threaded tasks, compression, and small-to-medium model LLM inference, it exhibits a performance tradeoff on larger models. LLM inference for a large 70B model drops to average speeds of 19.98 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 4.71 tokens/sec for text generation. Given the high memory density, dedicated CPU allocation, and local storage capacity, this instance is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, web serving, and medium-scale data processing.
description: Microsoft Azure E64ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores. It features 512.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio, and includes 3780 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, PassMark database operation scores, and memory metrics. Multi-core compute and memory bandwidth benchmarks rank in the top 25%. While it demonstrates top-tier performance in small-to-medium LLM inference, speed drops to average levels for large 70B models. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive databases, web serving, and data processing workloads requiring substantial local storage and high memory density.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64ads_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, high-capacity local storage, and top-tier database processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor
512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
3780 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU, memory, and database benchmark performance
Average performance on large-scale 70B LLM inference workloads
Suited for memory-intensive databases, web serving, and data processing
